findings.evidence: `rg -n "counit_map|map_comp_comul_apply" code/TauCeti/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/BaseChange.lean` hits lines 213 and 219; Mathlib already has `CoalgHomClass.counit_comp_apply` and `CoalgHomClass.map_comp_comul_apply` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/Hom.lean:80-87`, and `BialgHom` instances provide `CoalgHomClass` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/Hom.lean:122-126`.
findings.fix: Remove these two public lemmas; downstream users can use `CoalgHomClass.counit_comp_apply (map (K := K) f) x` and `CoalgHomClass.map_comp_comul_apply (map (K := K) f) x`, or the existing simp lemmas.
findings.issue: `counit_map` and `map_comp_comul_apply` expose scalar-extension-specific names for laws that every coalgebra hom already has, so they enlarge the public surface without characterizing base change.

summary: The base-change object, inclusion, tensor-operation formulas, functoriality, and antipode compatibility are exposed, but the file still exports generic coalgebra-hom preservation laws as TauCeti-specific API. Keep those facts available through the existing hom-class lemmas rather than adding public wrappers.
